quality_caveats.description: The local supervisor invoked the result-auditor subagent, but the runtime graph report states that the supervisor had to determine or repair decisions directly before validators passed.
The supervisor produced an initial audit report with wrong decisions for several items, then repaired the report after deterministic verifier feedback.

process_improvements_over_split_02: No runtime report delete command was observed.
The required graph report was written before the final marker.
Bridge reparse classified the run as accepted-candidate after benign parenthesized Test-Path checks were allowed.
remaining_issues: The supervisor still needed deterministic verifier feedback to repair subagent or self-derived decisions.
The bridge can prove that the subagent tool was invoked, but it does not yet independently score the quality of the subagent response.

token_costs.reason: This rerun was launched before a dedicated start/end supervisor-token checkpoint was recorded. Do not use this summary for cost economics.

interpretation: The no-delete and mandatory-graph-report hardening changed the same split from valid artifact with protocol failure into an accepted bridge candidate. The result supports graph-derived Copilot supervisor execution for five-artifact batches, but also shows that deterministic validators remain essential because the local supervisor/subagent lane still repairs incorrect initial decisions.
